steps.title: Read the constraint and the graph values
steps.content: The constraint: P3 gave equal actual ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The graph shows the model's correlation with P3's actual ratings was 0.30 for cubist paintings but only 0.16 for abstract paintings. If the model had predicted equal ratings for both styles, correlations with equal actual ratings would be equal — but they are not. The model must have predicted different ratings for the two styles.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "P3 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from cubist paintings than from abstract paintings.": Claims the model predicted a specific direction of difference, but the graph shows correlation strength, not which style was rated higher — an unsupported directional claim. ✗
  • "P3's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would equal one another.": Violates the logic rule — predicting equal ratings with equal actual ratings should yield equal correlations, but 0.30 ≠ 0.16. ✗
  • "P3 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from cubist paintings than from abstract paintings.": Same flaw as A — claims a direction the correlation data cannot support. ✗
  • "P3's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another.": The unequal correlations (0.30 vs. 0.16) with equal actual ratings can only result from the model having predicted different values for the two styles. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
